title: The Eisenhower Matrix
author: From: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
content: Distinguish between the urgent and the important. Most people spend their lives reacting to the urgent, leaving no time for the important.
action: Action: Move one task from 'urgent' to 'important' or delete it.

title: The Pareto Principle (80/20)
author: From: The 80/20 Principle
content: 80% of your results come from 20% of your efforts. Identify that 20% and double down.
action: Action: Identify the 20% of your work driving 80% of your value.

title: Single-Tasking
author: From: The Productivity Project
content: Multitasking is a myth; you are simply switching tasks rapidly and paying a 'switching cost' in cognitive performance.
action: Action: Set a timer for 60 minutes of single-tasking.

title: The 2-Minute Rule
author: From: Getting Things Done
content: If a task takes less than two minutes, do it immediately. Don't archive it, don't defer it.
action: Action: Clear your inbox of all 'under 2-minute' tasks.

title: Energy Management
author: From: The Power of Full Engagement
content: Time is finite; energy is renewable. Manage your energy, not your time.
action: Action: Schedule your most demanding task for your peak energy hour.
